```python
# test_camel_to_snake.py

import pytest

# Replace `your_module` with the actual module name where camel_to_snake is defined.
from your_module import camel_to_snake


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("input_value", "expected"),
    [
        ("camelCase", "camel_case"),
        ("CamelCase", "camel_case"),
        ("longCamelCaseString", "long_camel_case_string"),
        ("simpleTest", "simple_test"),
        ("alreadyLowercase", "already_lowercase"),
    ],
)
def test_camel_to_snake_happy_path(input_value, expected):
    assert camel_to_snake(input_value) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("input_value", "expected"),
    [
        ("", ""),
        ("a", "a"),
        ("A", "a"),
        ("already_snake", "already_snake"),
        ("ABC", "abc"),
        ("HTTPRequest", "httprequest"),
        ("getHTTPResponse", "get_httpresponse"),
        ("version2Value", "version2value"),
        ("versionValue2", "version_value2"),
        ("hello World", "hello world"),
    ],
)
def test_camel_to_snake_edge_cases(input_value, expected):
    assert camel_to_snake(input_value) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "invalid_input",
    [
        None,
        123,
        12.34,
        [],
        {},
        b"camelCase",
    ],
)
def test_camel_to_snake_invalid_input_raises_type_error(invalid_input):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        camel_to_snake(invalid_input)
```
